assignments.rationale: The note explicitly documents "laparoscopic cholecystectomy" and the procedure list states "Laparoscopic cholecystectomy on 06/18/2026 (primary procedure)." This matches the candidate for laparoscopic cholecystectomy without exploration.
"Incisional pain controlled on oral acetaminophen with oxycodone PRN" is acute post-procedural pain after surgery. This is more specific than unspecified pain.
The note documents "Obesity, BMI 33." A BMI of 33 falls in class 1 obesity, which is more specific than unspecified obesity.

assignments.description: Migraine without aura, not intractable, without status migrainosus
assignments.rationale: The past medical history states "Migraine without aura" and does not mention intractable symptoms, status migrainosus, or chronic migraine. This supports migraine without aura, not intractable, without status migrainosus.

assignments.fact: Status post laparoscopic cholecystectomy for acute calculous cholecystitis
assignments.description: Encounter for other specified surgical aftercare
assignments.rationale: The assessment documents postoperative care: "Status post laparoscopic cholecystectomy ... tolerating recovery well" on post-op day 2. The best matching aftercare code is encounter for other specified surgical aftercare.

assignments.description: Calculus of gallbladder with acute cholecystitis without obstruction
assignments.rationale: "Acute calculous cholecystitis" corresponds to calculus of gallbladder with acute cholecystitis without obstruction, since no obstruction is documented anywhere in the note.

The note states "Type 2 diabetes mellitus, currently well-controlled" and does not document complications or hyperglycemia requiring a more specific diabetes code. "Glucose 124" is not enough by itself to code hyperglycemia.
The assessment explicitly lists "Pain, post-procedural, controlled." This is acute postprocedural pain.

assignments.description: [SYNTHETIC] Ultrasound abdomen complete real-time with image documentation
assignments.rationale: The admission imaging is described as "Abdominal ultrasound" and "Operative ultrasound on admission." This matches ultrasound abdomen complete real-time with image documentation.

verdicts.note: Evidence clearly documents "laparoscopic cholecystectomy" performed on 06/18/2026 for acute calculous cholecystitis; no mention of common bile duct exploration, supporting the 'without exploration' variant.

Evidence explicitly states "Abdominal ultrasound" and "Operative ultrasound on admission documented gallbladder wall thickening with multiple stones," consistent with complete real-time ultrasound with image documentation.
